description: R implementations for analysis methods. Migrated from stato_r_implementation fields.

M.PairedTTest.callTemplate: t.test(`<response>` ~ <fixed_effect>, data = <dataset>, paired = TRUE, var.equal = FALSE, conf.level = <conf_level>)
M.PairedTTest.notes: Paired t-test via formula interface. The repeated_subject pairing is implicit in data ordering or handled via the paired=TRUE argument.

M.OneSampleTTest.notes: One-sample t-test. Tests whether the mean of x differs from mu.

M.TwoSampleTTest.callTemplate: t.test(`<response>` ~ <fixed_effect>, data = <dataset>, paired = FALSE, var.equal = FALSE, alternative = <alternative>, conf.level = <conf_level>)
M.TwoSampleTTest.notes: Two-sample (Welch) t-test via formula interface. fixed_effect must have exactly two levels.

M.FisherExactTest.notes: Fisher's exact test for 2x2 contingency tables. Both response and fixed_effect must be categorical/binary.

M.Count.callTemplate: result <- aggregate(`<response>` ~ <group>, data = <dataset>, FUN = function(x) sum(!is.na(x)))
M.Count.notes: Count of non-missing values via aggregate() with formula interface. <fixed_effect> expands to Treatment + Visit (etc.) via the engine's ' + ' role separator.

M.Mean.callTemplate: result <- aggregate(`<response>` ~ <group>, data = <dataset>, FUN = function(x) mean(x, na.rm = TRUE))
M.Mean.notes: Grouped arithmetic mean via aggregate() with formula interface.

M.StandardDeviation.callTemplate: result <- aggregate(`<response>` ~ <group>, data = <dataset>, FUN = function(x) sd(x, na.rm = TRUE))
M.StandardDeviation.notes: Grouped sample standard deviation via aggregate() with formula interface.

M.Median.callTemplate: result <- aggregate(`<response>` ~ <group>, data = <dataset>, FUN = function(x) median(x, na.rm = TRUE))
M.Median.notes: Grouped median via aggregate() with formula interface.

M.Minimum.callTemplate: result <- aggregate(`<response>` ~ <group>, data = <dataset>, FUN = function(x) { v <- x[!is.na(x)]; if (length(v) == 0) NA_real_ else min(v) })
M.Minimum.notes: Grouped minimum via aggregate() with formula interface. The wrapper guards against empty all-NA groups (min(numeric(0)) warns with Inf).

M.Maximum.callTemplate: result <- aggregate(`<response>` ~ <group>, data = <dataset>, FUN = function(x) { v <- x[!is.na(x)]; if (length(v) == 0) NA_real_ else max(v) })
M.Maximum.notes: Grouped maximum via aggregate() with formula interface. The wrapper guards against empty all-NA groups.

M.ANCOVA.callTemplate:
model <- lm(`<response>` ~ <covariate> + <fixed_effect>, data = <dataset>)
model_summary <- summary(model)
anova_result <- car::Anova(model, type = "<ss_type>")
lsm <- emmeans::emmeans(model, ~ <fixed_effect>)
lsm_df <- as.data.frame(summary(lsm, infer = TRUE, level = 1 - <alpha>))
contr <- pairs(lsm, adjust = "<multiplicity_adjustment>")
contr_df <- as.data.frame(summary(contr, infer = TRUE, level = 1 - <alpha>))
M.ANCOVA.notes: ANCOVA via lm() with Type III SS from car::Anova() and LS means from emmeans. Covariate adjusts the group comparison.

M.Subtraction.notes: Row-wise subtraction: difference = minuend - subtrahend. Used by T.ChangeFromBaseline (Change = current value - baseline value). Operates on concept-keyed columns.

M.Division.callTemplate:
num <- as.numeric(concept_data[["<numerator>"]])
den <- as.numeric(concept_data[["<denominator>"]])
concept_data[["<quotient>"]] <- ifelse(den != 0 & !is.na(den), num / den, NA_real_)
M.Division.notes: Row-wise division: quotient = numerator / denominator. Handles division by zero (returns NA). Used by T.ADAS_Cog_11_TotalScore (total = sum_available / capacity_fraction).

M.Aggregation.notes: Aggregation within partition groups. Config <agg_func> selects function (sum, count, mean, max, min). Result is merged back as a new column. Used by ADAS-Cog sub-derivations (sum of available scores, non-missing count, missing scale capacity, item max score).

M.ThresholdCompare.callTemplate:
val <- as.numeric(concept_data[["<value>"]])
thresh <- <threshold>
op <- "<operator>"
true_val <- "<true_value>"
false_val <- "<false_value>"
cmp <- switch(op,
  ">="  = val >= thresh,
  ">"   = val > thresh,
  "<="  = val <= thresh,
  "<"   = val < thresh,
  "=="  = val == thresh,
  "!="  = val != thresh,
  val >= thresh
)
concept_data[["<result>"]] <- ifelse(cmp, true_val, false_val)
M.ThresholdCompare.notes: Row-wise threshold comparison producing a flag column. Used by T.ADAS_ScoreableCheck (checks if non-missing count meets minimum threshold for scoring).

M.WindowedVisitAssignment.callTemplate:
# Windowed visit assignment. Reads a pre-computed study day (SDTM --DY,
# concept-keyed as <day>) and for each row finds the scheduled visit whose
# window contains that day. Ties broken by |day - targetDay| then min visitNumber.
# <day> → concept-keyed column name (e.g. 'Timing', materialised from QSDY).
# <analysisVisit>, <analysisVisitNumber>, <analysisDay> → output columns.
# configs$windows is a list of list(label, visitNumber, dayMin, dayMax, targetDay).
windows_def <- configs$windows
if (is.null(windows_def) || length(windows_def) == 0) stop("M.WindowedVisitAssignment: configs$windows is empty.")
day_raw <- concept_data[["<day>"]]
if (is.null(day_raw)) stop(sprintf("M.WindowedVisitAssignment: missing column '<day>'. have=%s", paste(colnames(concept_data), collapse=", ")))
day_num <- suppressWarnings(as.numeric(day_raw))
# Normalise windows_def → vectors for faster lookups.
w_label <- vapply(windows_def, function(w) as.character(w$label %||% ""), character(1))
w_vnum  <- vapply(windows_def, function(w) { v <- w$visitNumber; if (is.null(v)) NA_real_ else as.numeric(v) }, numeric(1))
w_min   <- vapply(windows_def, function(w) { v <- w$dayMin; if (is.null(v)) -Inf else as.numeric(v) }, numeric(1))
w_max   <- vapply(windows_def, function(w) { v <- w$dayMax; if (is.null(v))  Inf else as.numeric(v) }, numeric(1))
w_tgt   <- vapply(windows_def, function(w) { v <- w$targetDay; if (is.null(v)) NA_real_ else as.numeric(v) }, numeric(1))
assign_one <- function(d) {
  if (is.na(d)) return(c(NA_character_, NA_real_))
  hits <- which(d >= w_min & d <= w_max)
  if (length(hits) == 0) return(c(NA_character_, NA_real_))
  if (length(hits) > 1) {
    dist <- abs(d - w_tgt[hits])
    hits <- hits[dist == min(dist, na.rm = TRUE)]
    if (length(hits) > 1) {
      hits <- hits[which.min(w_vnum[hits])]
    }
  }
  c(w_label[hits], w_vnum[hits])
}
assigned <- vapply(day_num, assign_one, character(2))
concept_data[["<analysisVisit>"]]       <- assigned[1, ]
concept_data[["<analysisVisitNumber>"]] <- suppressWarnings(as.numeric(assigned[2, ]))
concept_data[["<analysisDay>"]]         <- day_num
M.WindowedVisitAssignment.notes: Row-wise visit assignment on a pre-computed study day (<day>, e.g. the Timing concept = SDTM QSDY). Reads configs$windows (not a template placeholder — arrays can't be stringified, so they're bound directly to the R env). Output columns are concept-keyed (<analysisVisit> substituted with e.g. 'AnalysisVisit'). `%||%` is defined in acdc_engine.R. The referenceBC method config is metadata only — the SAP anchor is already encoded in --DY at ingest time.

M.UnitConversion.callTemplate:
# Row-wise unit conversion operating on concept-keyed columns.
# Placeholders <value>, <source_unit>, <result_value>, <result_unit>
# are substituted with concept names (e.g. 'Observation.Result.Value')
# which ARE the internal dataframe column names after the load phase.
uc <- unit_conversions$conversions
target <- "<target_unit>"
precision <- <precision>
rounding_method <- "<rounding_method>"
on_missing <- "<on_missing_factor>"
factor_for <- function(src) {
  for (row in uc) {
    if ((tolower(row$from_code) == tolower(src) || tolower(row$from_name) == tolower(src)) &&
        (tolower(row$to_code) == tolower(target) || tolower(row$to_name) == tolower(target))) return(row$factor)
  }
  return(NA_real_)
}
src <- as.character(concept_data[["<source_unit>"]])
val <- as.numeric(concept_data[["<value>"]])
f <- vapply(src, factor_for, numeric(1))
missing_idx <- which(is.na(f) & !is.na(val))
if (length(missing_idx) > 0) {
  msg <- sprintf("Missing unit conversion factor for source values: %s (target=%s)",
                 paste(unique(src[missing_idx]), collapse = ", "), target)
  if (on_missing == "fail") stop(msg)
  if (on_missing == "na") val[missing_idx] <- NA_real_
  if (on_missing == "passthrough") f[missing_idx] <- 1
}
result_value <- val * f
if (!is.null(precision) && !is.na(precision)) {
  result_value <- switch(rounding_method,
    half_up   = round(result_value, precision),
    half_even = round(result_value, precision),
    half_down = sign(result_value) * floor(abs(result_value) * 10^precision + 0.5 - 1e-9) / 10^precision,
    floor     = floor(result_value * 10^precision) / 10^precision,
    ceiling   = ceiling(result_value * 10^precision) / 10^precision,
    round(result_value, precision)
  )
}
concept_data[["<result_value>"]] <- result_value
concept_data[["<result_unit>"]]  <- target
# Stamp the endpoint-defined Parameter label on every converted row so the
# downstream analysis cube can filter by Parameter == <parameter_label>.
concept_data[["Parameter"]] <- "<parameter_label>"
M.UnitConversion.notes: Generic row-wise unit conversion. Reads from concept-keyed columns <value> and <source_unit>, writes concept-keyed columns <result_value> and <result_unit>. Substitution of placeholders with concept names (e.g. Observation.Result.Value, Measure.Result.Value) is done by the engine's execute phase based on the transformation's bindings. Config placeholders (<target_unit>, <precision>, <rounding_method>, <on_missing_factor>) are substituted from the endpoint's resolved configurationValues. Requires `unit_conversions` (loaded from model/vocabularies/unit_conversions.json) and `concept_data` (the concept-keyed internal representation after the load phase) to be present in the R eval env. derivationKind='rowwise_mutate' tells the engine this is a pre-analysis dataframe mutation, not an analysis aggregation.
